verification.title: Verification Required
verification.description: To start earning money, you must be verified. Please follow these rules carefully so we can approve you without delays.
verification.selfie_rule_1: Upload a clear selfie where your face is fully visible and in focus.
verification.selfie_rule_2: Use good lighting, no strong filters or makeup that changes your appearance.
verification.selfie_rule_3: Only you should be in the frame. No other people or faces allowed.
verification.id_rule_1: If you use a passport booklet, upload the first page with your photo, full name, place of birth, and other main data.
verification.id_rule_2: If you use an ID card, upload the front side with your photo, full name, and main data. All info must be clearly visible and not blurry.
verification.ai_analyzing: 🔍 AI is analyzing your photos... This may take a moment.
verification.approved: ✅ Verification Approved! You can now start earning.
verification.inconclusive: ⏳ AI verification is inconclusive. An admin will review manually within 24 hours.
verification.unavailable: ⚠️ AI Verification Unavailable. An admin will review manually within 24 hours.
verification.error_selfie_type: Please select an image file for your selfie
verification.error_both_required: Please upload both selfie and ID photos
verification.error_upload_failed: Error uploading files. Please try again.
verification.footer_note: Your verification is usually reviewed within 24 hours. You won't be able to earn money until your account is verified. We will notify you once it's approved or if we need you to upload new materials.
verification.ai_error_no_face: We could not clearly see your face on the {{source}}. Make sure your face is fully visible, well lit and not blurred.
verification.ai_error_multiple_faces: We detected multiple faces on your {{source}}. Only you must be visible in the frame.
verification.ai_error_technical: Technical error while analyzing the document
verification.ai_error_id_rules: ID photo does not meet verification rules{{error}}
verification.ai_error_gender_mismatch: AI detected gender as {{gender}}. Sending to admin for manual verification.
verification.ai_error_cross_check: Mismatch: person on selfie ({{selfieGender}}) does not match person in ID ({{photoGender}}).
verification.ai_error_low_confidence: AI confidence is {{confidence}}% (minimum {{min}}% required for auto-approval). Sending to admin for manual review.
verification.ai_success_auto: AI verification passed automatically with high confidence based on your photos.

games.title: Mini Games
games.quiz_desc: Harry Potter & Lord of the Rings

killer_game.intro_desc: Solve mysteries together. Discuss the clues and make your guesses. The most attentive one wins!
killer_game.pick_prompt: Who is the killer? (tap when you are sure)

cases.title: Case 1: The Sunday Murder
cases.story: A rich man was found dead on Sunday morning. His wife said she was sleeping. The butler was polishing silver. The chef was making breakfast. The maid said she was getting the mail. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The Maid. There is no mail delivery on Sunday!

cases.title: Case 2: The Poisoned Drink
cases.story: Two girls ordered identical iced drinks. One drank hers very fast (in 10 seconds), and the other drank slowly. The one who drank slowly died of poisoning. Who or what killed her?
cases.explanation: The poison was inside the ice. The first girl drank her drink before the ice could melt.

cases.title: Case 3: Rain and the Gardener
cases.story: The cottage owner was found dead in the living room. Was a heavy rainstorm. The maid was dusting. The chef was cutting meat. The gardener said he was watering the plants outside. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The Gardener. It makes no sense to water plants outside during a heavy rainstorm.

cases.title: Case 4: Flight to Nowhere
cases.story: A billionaire was found dead on a private flight. The pilot was flying, the flight attendant was serving drinks, the business partner was sleeping, and the doctor was reading. Who poisoned his drink?
cases.explanation: The Doctor. Only he had access to medical vials to poison the drink unnoticed.

cases.title: Case 5: Curtains for the Star
cases.story: The lead actor was killed on stage during a sudden blackout. The director was watching, the understudy was changing into costume, the actress was waiting in the wings, and the lighting tech 'accidentally' cut the lights. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The Lighting Tech. He cut the lights and knew exactly where the actor was standing in the dark.

cases.title: Case 6: Modern Liner
cases.story: During a journey on a modern liner, a woman was pushed overboard in the middle of the night. The Captain was at the helm. The Husband was at the gym. The Photographer said he was in the darkroom developing digital photos. The Waitress was serving drinks. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The Photographer. Digital photos do not have film and do not need to be developed in a darkroom.

cases.title: Case 7: The Snowy Cabin
cases.story: A man was found hanged in an empty cabin. The door was locked from inside, with no furniture around and only a puddle of water under him. Three people were nearby: a stranger seeking shelter from the blizzard, a neighbor from the nearest house who heard a noise, and a visiting friend. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: Himself. He stood on a massive block of ice that eventually melted away.

cases.title: Case 8: The Chemist's Code
cases.story: A famous chemist was murdered. Before dying, he wrote elements on a paper: Nickel, Carbon, Potassium. The suspects: Nicholas, Edward, Felix.
cases.explanation: Nicholas. The chemical symbols: Ni (Nickel), C (Carbon), K (Potassium) form the name Ni-C-K.

cases.title: Case 9: The Mirror's Time
cases.story: The estate owner was killed in a hall with a large wall clock and a huge mirror opposite it. Before dying, he pointed at the number 10 in the clock's reflection. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: Butler. In a mirror reflection, the number 2 on a clock face appears as 10.

cases.title: Case 10: Prison Riot
cases.story: During a prison riot, the lights suddenly went out. When the power was restored, one of the guards was found dead. Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The inmate with a life sentence. He has nothing to lose, unlike the others who were going to be released.

cases.title: Case 11: Mansion Murder
cases.story:
The lights suddenly went out in the mansion. When they came back on, the owner was found dead in his study. The detective began an inspection of the house and those present:

Cook: Was in the kitchen, his apron covered in red stains. He explained: 'I was just cutting beets for dinner when everything went dark.'

Wife: Was in her room. She said: 'I was scared of the dark and immediately called a friend. We talked until the lights came back on.' The detective noticed her phone screen was shattered.

Barber: Was waiting in the hallway near the study. The detective saw sharp professional scissors in his pants pocket. The barber excused himself: 'I was just about to give the man a haircut. When the lights went out, I just froze in place from surprise.'

Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The wife. She claimed she was talking with a friend while it was dark. However, her phone was shattered during a struggle with her husband and was not working. She made up the story about the call as an alibi, not considering that the broken device would give her away.

cases.title: Case 12: Library Mystery
cases.story:
A man's body was found in the library. The detective interviewed everyone in the house: the wife, the best friend, the gardener, and the cleaner.
Wife: 'I was in the kitchen, making tea. My husband asked me not to disturb him because he had finally found a rare book and wanted to study it.'
Best Friend: 'We argued about money this morning, so I went to the garden. I saw him through the window sitting in his armchair, but I didn't go inside.'
Cleaner: 'I went into the library to put a glass of water on the table. The master didn't even look up — he was so absorbed in studying the text on pages 21 and 22 that he didn't even notice me. I put the water down and left immediately.'
Gardener: 'I was trimming the bushes under the windows. It was quiet in the library; I only heard him turn a page.'
Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The cleaner. In most books, an odd-numbered page and the next even-numbered page (like 21 and 22) are printed on two sides of the same leaf. They cannot be visible at the same time when the book is open.

cases.title: Case 13: Night Museum
cases.story:
A museum night guard was found dead near the vault with valuable exhibits. The security cameras were disabled at 10:15 PM. The detective questioned everyone who remained in the building:
Curator: 'I was in my office cataloguing new acquisitions. My office is soundproof — I heard nothing until I came out at 11 PM and found the body.'
Restorer: 'I was working on a 17th century painting in the restoration room. I wore gloves all evening and never left my area.'
Electrician: 'I was fixing the wiring in the basement. I was down there all evening — only came up when I heard screaming. I don't know why the cameras went off, maybe it was because of my work on the wiring.'
Cleaning lady: 'I was vacuuming the main hall. When I turned off the vacuum to take a break, I heard a thud and ran towards the sound.'
Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The electrician. Only he had access to the electrical panel to disable the security cameras at 10:15 PM, then used the moment for the murder. His excuse that the cameras went off 'because of his wiring work' only confirms he knew about the shutdown.

cases.title: Case 14: Fake Date
cases.story:
A famous banker was found shot in his office. The detective questioned the suspects:
Wife: 'We were planning a vacation in July. These last few days he wasn't himself, constantly writing something in his office. I thought he was preparing documents, but he was probably saying goodbye...'
Brother: 'I came to him in the evening to ask for money, but the door was locked. I heard a paper shredder working inside. I thought he was busy and left.'
Secretary: 'I spent all day preparing June reports for the tax office. The boss ordered me to check every document so that not a single date was wrong. I was just helping him with the last report for June 31st, and then I left. He was still alive.'
Who is the killer?
cases.explanation: The secretary. June only has 30 days, so the date 'June 31st' in the note reveals the killer who tried to stage a suicide.

cases.title: Case 15: The Robbery
cases.story:
The jewelry store alarm went off in the middle of the night. Police arrived to find a smashed display window and empty stands. The detective questioned everyone:
Owner: 'I got a call at 3 AM. I drove over and saw this horror — the window was shattered, the jewelry gone. I had everything insured, but it was my life's work...'
Guard: 'I stepped away for 10 minutes to get coffee from a vending machine around the corner. When I came back, the window was already broken and someone had rummaged through the inside.'
Suspected thief: 'I was just walking by and saw the broken window. I peeked inside out of curiosity — and then the police showed up. I was arrested for nothing!'
Bystander: 'I saw a shadow throw something at the window from the street. The glass shattered and the shadow climbed inside.'
The detective carefully examined the scene and noticed that ALL the glass shards were lying on the sidewalk in front of the store. Who really committed the crime?
cases.explanation: The owner. If the window had been broken from the outside, the shards would have fallen inside the store. Since they were on the sidewalk — the window was broken from inside to fake a robbery and collect the insurance.
